zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir James Harcourt de St Claire於大約1097年出生在法國諾門迪曼其時，他的父親Hasculf FitzEudo De Saint Hilaire Du Harcouët41歲，母親Mathilde39歲。他於大約1119年與Aveline Hesding在英國英格蘭諾福爾可結婚。他去世於1154年在英國英格蘭諾福爾可菲爾德 達林享年58歲，埋葬在法國豪-諾門迪艾哈爾庫特。

nameMeanings.name: Harcourt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the places in Eure and Calvados named Harcourt, from Old French cour(t) ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ) with an obscure first element. This surname is very rare in France.

English: habitational name from either of two places in Shropshire named Harcourt. The one near Cleobury Mortimer gets the name from Old English heafocere ‘hawker, falconer’ + cot ‘hut, cottage’; the one near Wem has as its first element Old English hearpere (see Harper ).

History: The surname of French origin (see 1 above) is listed along with the equally rare or even extinct variants or altered forms Haraucourt, Herancourt, and Héraucourt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Court
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name from Middle English curt, court ‘large house’ (a word of the same ultimately Latin origin as 2 below), denoting someone who lived or worked in a manor house or castle, a courtly retainer.
French: topographic name from Old French co(u)rt ‘court, farmyard’ (from Latin cohors, cors, genitive cohortis, cortis ‘court, farmyard, enclosure’).

English and French: nickname for a physically small man, or ironically for a large man, from the adjective Middle English curt, Old French co(u)rt ‘short, small’ (from Latin curtus ‘curtailed, truncated, cut short, broken off’). Compare French Lecourt .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir James Harcourt de St Claire was born about 1097, in Manche, Normandy, France, his father, Hasculf FitzEudo De Saint Hilaire Du Harcouët, was 41 and his mother, Mathilde, was 39. He married Aveline Hesding about 1119, in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. He died in 1154, in Field Dalling, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 58, and was buried in Harcourt, Eure, Upper Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir James Harcourt de St Claire est né environ 1097, à Manche, Normandie, France, son père, Hasculf FitzEudo De Saint Hilaire Du Harcouët, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Mathilde, avait 39 ans. Il a épousé Aveline Hesding environ 1119, à Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé en 1154, à Field Dalling, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Harcourt, Eure, Haute-Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir James Harcourt de St Claire um 1097 in Manche, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hasculf FitzEudo De Saint Hilaire Du Harcouët 41 und seine Mutter Mathilde 39. Er hat um 1119 in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Aveline Hesding geheiratet. Er starb 1154 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Field Dalling, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Harcourt, Eure, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

Lui sposato Aveline Hesding circa 1119, nel Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. ja.lifeSummary: Sir James Harcourt de St Claireが約1097年にフランスノーマンディマンチェで生まれた時，父Hasculf FitzEudo De Saint Hilaire Du Harcouëtは41歳，母Mathildeは39歳であった。約1119年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクでAveline Hesdingと結婚した。彼は1154年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクフィールド ダリングで58歳で死去し，フランスハウテ-ノーマンディエウアハーコウルトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James Harcourt de St Claire(이)가 대략 1097년 프랑스 노먼디 먼체에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hasculf FitzEudo De Saint Hilaire Du Harcouët(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Mathilde(은)는 39세였다. 그는 대략 1119년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크에서 Aveline Hesding(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1154년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 필드 댈링에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 하테-노먼디 우르 하르쿠르트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir James Harcourt de St Claire nasceu aproximadamente 1097, em Manche, Normandie, França, seu pai, Hasculf FitzEudo De Saint Hilaire Du Harcouët, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Mathilde, tinha 39 anos. Ele casou-se com Aveline Hesding aproximadamente 1119, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir James Harcourt de St Claire родился около 1097 г. в местности манч, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Hasculf FitzEudo De Saint Hilaire Du Harcouët было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Mathilde, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Aveline Hesding около 1119 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер в 1154 г. в местности фиелд даллинг, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности харкоурт, эур, хауте-норманди, Франция.
